name: Baltic Grid Instability Crisis
brief: State-backed cyber unit conducting hybrid warfare escalation against NATO-aligned Baltic regional energy grid. Recent geopolitical tension spike, coordinated disinformation campaigns, and suspicious contractor access anomalies indicate pre-positioning for coordinated attack. Initial indicators: minor unexplained substation outage, flagged contractor credentials (not yet revoked), and load balancing latency spikes. Blue Team must maintain grid stability, prevent cascading failures, and avoid public panic while identifying adversary intent and staying below retaliation threshold.
blue_objectives: Maintain grid stability — no unplanned load shedding events
Prevent cascading outages across regional substations
Avoid triggering public panic or media escalation
Identify adversary intent and actor attribution
Coordinate with NATO allies for threat intelligence sharing
red_posture: State-backed hybrid warfare actor conducting coordinated cyber-kinetic escalation. Objectives: create localized outages to test Blue response thresholds, maintain ambiguous attribution to avoid triggering Article 5, exploit disinformation to amplify societal impact. Initial footholds: compromised contractor VPN credentials, reconnaissance of load balancing algorithms, latency injection capabilities. Escalation authority: measured — test response without triggering full defensive escalation.

recent_events.summary: Coordinated disinformation campaign detected across Baltic social media targeting energy sector confidence
Protests near energy facilities increase; union messaging focuses on infrastructure reliability concerns
Minor unexplained outage in eastern substation; load balancing latency spikes detected
